title: salix exigua subsp. interior
intro: Salix exigua subsp. interior, also known as the narrowleaf cottonwood, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to North America, characterized by its narrow, lance-shaped leaves and cotton-like seeds. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be beneficial for treating wounds, fever, and various infections. The leaves and bark of the narrowleaf cottonwood have been used to make herbal preparations, including teas and infusions, which can help to reduce inflammation, alleviate cold and flu symptoms, and promote digestive health. Additionally, the plant's antimicrobial properties make it a popular ingredient in natural remedies for skin conditions and respiratory issues.
